<!-- PROSE — edit freely in Obsidian -->

A satirical comedy originally written for a family performance at Yasnaya Polyana. An aristocratic household is gripped by a fashionable obsession with spiritualism and séances, while three peasants wait in the hallway hoping to buy a parcel of land. The servants — sharper and more practical than their masters — ultimately manipulate the séance to get the peasants what they need. Tolstoy uses the comedy to expose the idleness and self-deception of the educated class against the grounded pragmatism of the peasantry.
